Title: Functional Characterization of Four Olive Squalene Synthases with Respect to the Squalene Content of the Virgin Olive Oil
Journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Squalene biosynthesis entry step to sterol/triterpene pathway
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Isoprenoid biosynthesis class 1 superfamily; squalene/phytoene synthase domain (Pfam 00494)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two molecules of farnesyl pyrophosphate to presqualene diphosphate and then converts it to squalene in the presence of NADPH and Mg2+.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed step; upstream entry to sterol/triterpene branch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher than OeSQS3/OeSQS4 in several tissues; highest in Arbequina young seeds and in leaves of both cultivars; low and steady in mesocarp development of Picual and Arbequina; upregulated in water-stressed Arbequina mesocarp during later development/ripening
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Young seeds; leaves; low steady expression in mesocarp; induced under deficit irrigation at later stages in Arbequina mesocarp
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit development and ripening; regulated deficit irrigation/water stress (FI, 60RDI, 30RDI)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Full-length cDNA cloning; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli; in vitro enzyme assay with FPP/NADPH/Mg2+; GC and GC-MS product identification; qRT-PCR expression profiling; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OepSQS1 encodes a functional squalene synthase confirmed by bacterial expression and in vitro formation of squalene. 2. It is predicted to be ER-localized and contains C-terminal transmembrane domains typical of plant SQS. 3. Its transcript abundance is generally lower than OeSQS2 in olive mesocarp, so it is not the main determinant of mesocarp/oil squalene under normal conditions. 4. Under deficit irrigation in Arbequina mesocarp, OeSQS1 is upregulated during later stages, coinciding with recovery of squalene content.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to squalene biosynthesis; may help preserve mesocarp squalene content under water stress at ripening stages, but is not the principal gene for olive oil squalene accumulation under normal conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as main control step for oil squalene under normal conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker or engineering target for maintaining squalene under water-limited production; likely secondary to OeSQS2 for increasing virgin olive oil squalene.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutritionally valuable component of virgin olive oil; precursor of sterols and triterpenes; antioxidant role suggested in mature fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Coding sequence was expressed without the C-terminal ER retention/hydrophobic region to improve soluble recombinant expression. Leaves showed high transcript levels without corresponding squalene accumulation, suggesting roles linked to phytosterol/triterpene formation rather than free squalene accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from olive transcriptome and wild olive genome; no accession reported in provided text

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Squalene biosynthesis entry step to sterol/triterpene pathway
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Isoprenoid biosynthesis class 1 superfamily; squalene/phytoene synthase domain (Pfam 00494)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two molecules of farnesyl pyrophosphate to presqualene diphosphate and then reduces it to squalene using NADPH and Mg2+.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed step; upstream entry to sterol/triterpene branch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest transcript levels in mesocarp among olive SQS genes; also highly expressed in young seeds, leaves, young drupes, and Picual mesocarp; higher in Picual mesocarp than Arbequina; dominant transcript in Dokkar and Klon-14 mesocarp; downregulated at green stages under deficit irrigation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mesocarp-biased dominant SQS transcript during fruit development; cultivar-dependent temporal expression; reduced under water stress at green stage
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit development and ripening; cultivar comparison; regulated deficit irrigation/water stress (FI, 60RDI, 30RDI)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Full-length cDNA cloning; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli; in vitro enzyme assay with FPP/NADPH/Mg2+; GC and GC-MS product identification; qRT-PCR expression profiling across tissues, development, cultivars, and irrigation treatments; squalene quantification by GC
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OepSQS2 encodes a functional squalene synthase. 2. It shows the highest transcript abundance in olive mesocarp among the four SQS genes. 3. Higher OeSQS2 expression in Picual than Arbequina parallels higher mesocarp/oil squalene, supporting it as the main contributor to squalene biosynthesis in olive mesocarp and virgin olive oil. 4. OeSQS2 is also the predominant SQS transcript in Dokkar and Klon-14 mesocarp. 5. Deficit irrigation lowers OeSQS2 expression at green stages, consistent with reduced mesocarp squalene under water stress.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes squalene accumulation in olive mesocarp and is the principal gene associated with squalene content of virgin olive oil.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as the main contributing/control SQS gene for mesocarp squalene biosynthesis, but overall squalene level may also depend on downstream enzymes such as squalene epoxidase
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Primary candidate for marker-assisted selection and metabolic engineering to increase squalene content in virgin olive oil; useful breeding marker for high-squalene cultivars.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutritionally valuable component of virgin olive oil; precursor for sterol and triterpene biosynthesis; antioxidant role suggested in mature fruit.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although OeSQS2 best explains mesocarp squalene biosynthesis, transcript level alone did not fully correlate with contrasting cultivars Dokkar and Klon-14, implying additional control by downstream metabolism such as squalene epoxidase.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from olive transcriptome and wild olive genome; no accession reported in provided text

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Squalene biosynthesis entry step to sterol/triterpene pathway
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Isoprenoid biosynthesis class 1 superfamily; squalene/phytoene synthase domain (Pfam 00494)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of squalene from two molecules of farnesyl pyrophosphate in the presence of NADPH and Mg2+.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed step; upstream entry to sterol/triterpene branch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low expression in all studied tissues; no transcript detected in Arbequina mesocarp and leaves in tissue survey; low and steady in mesocarp development; very low in Dokkar and Klon-14 mesocarp; low/constant under deficit irrigation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Generally weakly expressed across tissues and fruit development
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit development and ripening; cultivar comparison; regulated deficit irrigation/water stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Full-length cDNA cloning; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli; in vitro enzyme assay with FPP/NADPH/Mg2+; GC and GC-MS product identification; qRT-PCR expression profiling; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OepSQS3 encodes a functional squalene synthase. 2. It is predicted to be ER-localized and contains C-terminal transmembrane domains. 3. Transcript levels are very low in most olive tissues and cultivars examined. 4. Its expression does not correlate with mesocarp squalene content, indicating a minor role in determining virgin olive oil squalene.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Minor contributor to squalene biosynthesis; little evidence that it determines mesocarp/oil squalene content.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Low priority target for increasing olive oil squalene relative to OeSQS2.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutritionally valuable component of virgin olive oil; precursor of sterols and triterpenes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Coding sequence was expressed without the C-terminal hydrophobic region for bacterial functional assay.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from olive transcriptome and wild olive genome; no accession reported in provided text

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Squalene biosynthesis entry step to sterol/triterpene pathway
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Isoprenoid biosynthesis class 1 superfamily; squalene/phytoene synthase domain (Pfam 00494); predicted chloroplast transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes NADPH- and Mg2+-dependent conversion of two molecules of farnesyl pyrophosphate to squalene.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed step; upstream entry to sterol/triterpene branch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Observed in young drupes and mesocarp, almost undetectable in seeds and leaves; very low in most mesocarp stages except a peak in Picual at 28 WAF; very low in Dokkar and Klon-14; low/variable under irrigation treatments
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Young drupes and mesocarp, but overall weak expression compared with OeSQS2
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit development and ripening; cultivar comparison; regulated deficit irrigation/water stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Full-length cDNA cloning; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli after removal of N-terminal chloroplast peptide; in vitro enzyme assay with FPP/NADPH/Mg2+; GC and GC-MS product identification; qRT-PCR expression profiling; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OepSQS4 encodes a functional squalene synthase despite being divergent from the other olive SQS proteins. 2. It lacks a transmembrane domain and is predicted to localize to the chloroplast due to an N-terminal transit peptide. 3. Transcript abundance is low in most tissues and largely does not track mesocarp squalene levels. 4. Therefore it is unlikely to be a major determinant of virgin olive oil squalene content.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely minor contributor to overall olive mesocarp squalene accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Low priority target for breeding high-squalene virgin olive oil compared with OeSQS2.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutritionally valuable component of virgin olive oil; precursor of sterols and triterpenes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Most divergent olive SQS; predicted chloroplast localization differs from the ER localization of OepSQS1-3.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from olive transcriptome and wild olive genome; no accession reported in provided text
